statement.p: When there is a 17.3\ {\textstyle\frac{\rm\mathstrut mi}{\rm\mathstrut hr}} wind, an airplane can fly 860\ {\rm mi} with the wind in the same time that it can fly 752\ {\rm mi} against the wind. Find the speed of the plane when there is no wind.
The plane's airspeed is when there is no wind.

solution.p: Assume the plane travels at x miles per hour when there is no wind. Its given that the wind speed is 17.3 miles per hour. Then, the plane flies at x+17.3 miles per hour with the wind, and at x-17.3 miles per hour against the wind.

Using the formula \text{time}=\frac{\text{distance}}{\text{speed}}, we know the plane can travel 860 miles with the wind in \frac{860}{x+17.3} hours; and it can travel 752 miles against the wind in \frac{752}{x-17.3} hours.
Its given that it takes the same time in both trips, so we can write an equation and solve it:
THe planes airspeed is {258.219\ {\textstyle\frac{\rm\mathstrut mi}{\rm\mathstrut hr}}} when there is no wind.
